field.label: Production Environment
field.comment:
Yes = this is your live site. The module checks the licence key against the current domain. If invalid, the module silently disables itself.
No = this is dev/staging. The module runs at full features without a licence — use this on non-standard dev domains the auto-detector misses.
Default: Yes.

field.label: License Key
field.comment:
Paste the key from your purchase email. Bound to the domain you provided at checkout.
Auto-bypassed for development hosts (localhost, *.test, *.local, staging.*, *.magento.cloud, ngrok, etc.) — no key needed there.
www. prefix is normalised — one key works for both shop.com and www.shop.com.

field.label: Enable Module
field.comment:
Master switch.
Yes = render ETA badges and the order-email injection where enabled under "Display Locations" below.
No = module silently does nothing on the storefront. Useful for quickly toggling all ETA rendering off without removing the configuration.

field.label: Default Restock Date
field.comment:
Fallback text used for sold-out / backorder products that have no per-product Restock Date value set. Combined with the Label Prefix below. Per-store-view configurable.
Examples: 5-7 business days, 2 weeks, next month.
Leave blank to suppress the fallback — products with no per-product value will then not display any badge at all.

field.label: Label Prefix (default text only)
field.comment:
Prefix prepended to the Default Restock Date only. Examples: Ships in, Available in, Restock:.
Per-product Restock Date values are shown verbatim with no prefix, so each product can have its own exact wording (e.g. "Ships 15 December 2026" or "Pre-order — January batch").

field.label: Show on Product Page
field.comment:
Yes = render the restock-date badge below the price on the product detail page for sold-out products. Renders on both Luma and Hyvä themes.
No = hide on the product page. The badge will still appear on cart / checkout / order email if those are enabled below.

field.label: Show on Cart Page
field.comment:
Yes = show the restock date next to each sold-out line item on the shopping cart page. Reassures customers before they commit to checkout.
No = hide on cart.

field.label: Show on Checkout
field.comment:
Yes = show the restock date next to sold-out line items in the checkout summary (Hyvä Checkout supported via a view-model + phtml block).
No = hide on checkout. Common choice if you only want the product page + cart to display restock dates.

field.label: Include in Order Confirmation Email
field.comment:
Yes = inject restock-date notes into sold-out line items in the order confirmation email (highest-engagement email any merchant sends).
No = no email modification. Custom-email merchants who've rewritten the items.phtml block should leave this Yes — the module is a non-destructive plugin and will silently skip if the template was replaced.

field.label: Badge Style
field.comment:
Visual treatment of the ETA badge:
Warning (amber) — best for "this might delay your order" tone.
Info (blue) — neutral, informational.
Neutral (grey) — minimal, blends with the design.
Both Luma and Hyvä templates honour this setting.

field.label: Hide PDP Badge When Product is Next Day Eligible
field.tooltip: Yes hides the "Ships in X business days" badge on product pages when the product is also flagged Next Day Eligible.
field.comment:
Only relevant if you also have etechflow/module-next-day-eligibility installed.

Resolves the contradiction where a product shows BOTH the green "Next Day Eligible" badge AND the amber "Ships in 5-7 business days" badge. The two messages confuse customers — one says fast, the other says slow.

Yes — on product pages, hide the restock-date badge when the product is Next Day Eligible. The green badge tells the story; the slow ETA is suppressed. Products that are backordered AND not next-day-eligible still show the restock date as normal.
No (default) — show both badges if both modules say to. Behaviour unchanged from earlier versions.

Only affects the product-page badge. The cart-page summary, checkout note, and order-confirmation email injection are unaffected — those still fire on any backorder line item regardless of next-day eligibility.

Soft-detected: if Next Day Eligibility isn't installed, this setting is a no-op (the attribute doesn't exist, the check falls through). Safe to leave on Yes even if you uninstall NDE later.
